name: Rosalia Code
title: Rosalia Code White Paper
author: Nicholas Van-Orton
core_thesis: Meaning is an effect produced by the organization of perception.
definition: Rosalia Code is a perception theory that examines how meaning emerges from the organization of visual, auditory, spatial and temporal elements within an experience.

origin.observation: The choreography functions as a perceptual system in which attention, movement, rhythm and spatial relations generate meaning.

principles.name: Perceptual Organization
principles.definition: Meaning emerges from the structure of perception rather than from isolated elements.

principles.name: Attention Guidance
principles.definition: The movement of attention determines how an experience is interpreted.

principles.name: Relational Meaning
principles.definition: Meaning is produced by relationships between elements rather than by elements alone.

principles.name: Experiential Construction
principles.definition: The observer participates in the creation of meaning through perception.

conclusion.statement: Meaning is not contained within objects themselves.
conclusion.final_principle: Meaning is an effect produced by the organization of perception.
